notes: Chunk 77 DONE (Template B, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + Blog Day 30. Closed the largest gap in the Maldives cluster opened in chunk 76: all 12 remaining hub-market EU/EEA destinations not yet paired with the Maldives (Germany, France, Italy, Netherlands, Spain, Portugal, Belgium, Austria, Switzerland, Czech Republic, Poland, Hungary), both directions, plus the missing Maldives-to-Canada leg (Canada-to-Maldives already existed). Every maldives-to-X page leads with a cat (the realistic enquiry, since the Maldives' own dog-import ban has left almost no resident dog population) but correctly states that a resident dog faces no legal bar to leaving, only entering; every X-to-maldives page restates the entry ban plainly, matching the established chunk-76 framing. All 12 EU-bound pages use the current EU legal basis (Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2026/131, Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/705, Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/636), reusing verified per-destination facts from sibling Lebanon-to-EU route files rather than re-deriving them. Switzerland (both directions) is a genuine open question handled honestly: FSVO only requires the titre test, 3-month wait and advance permission for pets from countries it classifies as high rabies risk, and we could not confirm the Maldives' specific listing on FSVO's own country list despite the Maldives being rabies-free/low-risk under comparable UK and US classifications, so both Switzerland pages state the ambiguity directly and give a wide 8-26 week range rather than a false single estimate. Maldives-to-Canada: verified CFIA's actual personal-pet-cat rule is markedly lighter than the EU/UK pathway, a rabies vaccination certificate only, no titre test, no 90-day wait, no import permit. Genuine differentiating facts verified per EU destination via web search: Austrian Airlines' non-stop Vienna-Male service (the only Gulf-free EU routing found); Edelweiss Air's seasonal direct Zurich-Male service; LOT Polish Airlines' scheduled direct Warsaw-Male service; Neos' seasonal direct Milan Malpensa-Male and Prague-Male charter services; Condor's seasonal Frankfurt-Male service; French Bee's newly announced Paris Orly-Male route launching 19 December 2026; the Air France-KLM-Martinair Cargo Animal Hotel at Schiphol handling NVWA border inspection on-site; Madrid-Barajas' four separate live-animal Border Control Posts across three ground handlers; Lisbon's Terminal 6 live-animal freight handled by CEAL Cargo; and, where no direct flight could be confirmed (Belgium, Hungary), Male's lack of a resident pet-shipping agent, with most bookings routed through IPATA-certified agents in Colombo, Sri Lanka. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block, correct Marcus Webb persona, no placeholder strings, no contact email/phone stated). Blog Day 30: iata-pet-crate-guide.md (new article, Marcus Webb, approximately 2,800 words), covering IATA crate sizing and construction rules. Caught and corrected a genuine, widespread site error while researching: the current IATA Live Animals Regulations (52nd edition, January 2026) place domestic dogs and cats under Container Requirement 1, not Container Requirement 82 as commonly assumed across the pet transport industry, including some existing pages on this site; CR82 actually governs wild canid/felid species (wolves, jackals, foxes, bobcats, lynx). Kept 'CR82' in the title/keywords for search intent (it is the term the market searches), but the body states the correct CR1 attribution honestly. Also corrected two other common errors: ventilation must cover all four sides at a minimum 16% of combined surface area (some existing site pages say three sides), and cable ties are permitted as a supplementary fastener, not banned outright, only forbidden as a sole/primary fastening method. Flagged below for a future correction pass: existing site pages that state 'CR82' or 'three-sided ventilation' for domestic pet crates should be corrected to match this research. Routes 6,346 -> 6,371, blog 431 -> 432, content plan articles 29 -> 30, pages 7,151 -> 7,177. Chunk 78 (Template C, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
